title: United States of China
dek: An analysis of China’s strategic posture in a fragmenting global system and the implications for capital allocators.
keyFindings: Geopolitical fragmentation is reshaping capital-flow assumptions.
Domestic-policy stability influences relative market attractiveness.
Credit allocation opportunities can emerge during strategic realignment.

paragraphs: This issue is presented in ShoreVest’s unified print format for consistency across the China Debt Dynamics archive.

disclaimer: This letter expresses the views of the author as of the date indicated and such views are subject to change without notice.
